claims.claim_text: Hubble is built on top of Cilium and eBPF as a network, service, and security observability platform for Kubernetes.
claims.rationale: The upstream Hubble README directly states that Hubble is built on Cilium and eBPF and describes its Kubernetes observability scope.

claims.claim_text: Cilium uses an eBPF-based dataplane and eBPF logic in Linux kernel hooks for networking, policy, and visibility behavior.
claims.rationale: The upstream Cilium README describes Cilium as using an eBPF-based dataplane and explains eBPF kernel integration points for networking, security, and visibility.

claims.claim_text: Cilium's Hubble integration provides service maps, flow visibility with identity and label metadata, DNS-aware filtering, protocol-specific insights, and drop reasons.
claims.rationale: The Cilium README's observability section directly lists Hubble service maps, identity and label metadata, DNS-aware filtering, protocol insights, and drop reasons.

claims.claim_text: The Hubble server runs on each node, is embedded in the Cilium agent, retrieves eBPF-based visibility from Cilium, and exposes gRPC flows and Prometheus metrics.
claims.rationale: The Cilium component overview directly describes the Hubble server's per-node placement, embedding in the Cilium agent, and gRPC/Prometheus exposure.

claims.claim_text: Hubble Relay connects to running Hubble servers and provides cluster-wide visibility and an API representing all servers in the cluster.
claims.rationale: The Cilium component overview directly states that Relay is aware of running Hubble servers and offers cluster-wide visibility through their gRPC APIs.

claims.claim_text: The Hubble CLI can connect to Hubble Relay or a local Hubble server to retrieve flow events.
claims.rationale: The Cilium component overview directly describes the Hubble CLI connection targets and its role in retrieving flow events.

claims.claim_text: Hubble UI provides service dependency and connectivity maps using Hubble data, including L3/L4 and L7 service-map visualization.
claims.rationale: The Hubble UI README directly describes automatic service dependency graph discovery and Service Map visualization at L3/L4 and L7.

claims.claim_text: Cilium Helm values include controls for enabling Hubble, Hubble Relay, Hubble UI, and Hubble metrics, with metric examples such as DNS, drop, TCP, flow, ICMP, and HTTP.
claims.rationale: The Cilium Helm values file documents hubble.enabled, hubble.relay.enabled, hubble.ui.enabled, and hubble.metrics.enabled with the same metric categories.

claims.claim_text: Cilium's Hubble setup workflow validates Hubble with cilium status, installs the Hubble CLI from release artifacts, and validates API access with hubble status showing health, flow counts, flows/s, and connected nodes.
claims.rationale: The upstream setup document directly includes cilium status validation, Hubble CLI installation, and hubble status output fields.

claims.claim_text: The Hubble CLI supports observing HTTP flows and filtering for dropped or policy-denied flows with hubble observe.
claims.rationale: The Hubble CLI guide directly shows protocol filtering, dropped-flow filtering, and policy-denied flow output.

claims.claim_text: A Kubernetes NetworkPolicy from entry that combines namespaceSelector and podSelector selects particular pods within particular namespaces; separate entries behave differently.
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes NetworkPolicy documentation directly explains combined namespaceSelector and podSelector semantics and contrasts them with separate from entries.

claims.claim_text: Cilium/Hubble Layer 7 visibility requires L7 proxy support and L7 CiliumNetworkPolicy rules; matching DNS and HTTP flows can then appear in hubble observe output.
claims.rationale: The Cilium L7 visibility documentation states the proxy and policy requirements and shows DNS/HTTP L7 flow details in hubble observe output.

claims.claim_text: Hubble metrics are exported under the hubble_ Prometheus namespace and include DNS, drop, flow, HTTP, ICMP, and TCP metric families.
claims.rationale: The Cilium metrics reference directly lists the hubble_ namespace and the relevant exported metric families.

claims.claim_text: Prometheus stores metrics as labeled time-series data and collects time series using a pull model over HTTP.
claims.rationale: The existing Prometheus overview source in the section pool directly describes labeled time series and HTTP pull collection.

claims.claim_text: Distributed tracing represents request work as traces and spans and helps inspect request paths across services.
claims.rationale: The existing OpenTelemetry traces source in the section pool directly explains traces, spans, and request paths across services.

claims.claim_text: Istio service mesh observability provides telemetry for service communications, including metrics, distributed traces, access logs, and Envoy proxy-level metrics.
claims.rationale: The Istio observability documentation directly lists metrics, traces, access logs, and proxy-level metrics generated by Envoy proxies.

claims.claim_text: Istio traffic management relies on Envoy proxies and supports routing rules, circuit breakers, timeouts, and retries.
claims.rationale: The Istio traffic management documentation directly describes Envoy-based proxying and traffic-management features including routing, circuit breakers, timeouts, and retries.

claims.claim_text: Istio security features include workload identity and transparent TLS encryption, with mutual TLS used for service security.
claims.rationale: The Istio security documentation directly describes strong identity, transparent TLS encryption, and mutual TLS as service security features.

further_reading.title: Hubble README
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary upstream overview of Hubble's purpose, features, flow visibility, service maps, and metrics.

further_reading.title: Cilium Component Overview
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the Cilium agent, Hubble server, Relay, CLI, UI, and eBPF roles used in the module architecture.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Network Policies
further_reading.why_relevant: Authoritative reference for NetworkPolicy selector behavior used in the policy debugging examples.

further_reading.title: Cilium Hubble Metrics Reference
further_reading.why_relevant: Lists Hubble metric families, labels, and Prometheus naming needed for the metrics section.

further_reading.title: Prometheus Overview
further_reading.why_relevant: Background on Prometheus time-series collection and pull-based monitoring for Hubble metric exports.

notes: No war_story, incident, pricing, benchmark, or statistic claims requiring rewrite were found. The module is primarily upstream capability claims and Kubernetes NetworkPolicy semantics.
